intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

body.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

draft.title: recipe app for people who hate cooking
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
recipe app for people who hate cooking

Building your first draft…

data.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

facade.hypothesis: Users want immediate feedback without animation delays—typing shows results instantly rather than with fade-in transitions
facade.axisTargeted: Speed of response: instant vs animated
facade.label: Instant search results
facade.acceptImplies: The app should prioritize snappy, zero-latency interactions; people who hate cooking need friction removed immediately
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer polished transitions and breathing room in the UI; animations signal refinement and reduce cognitive load from instant change

evidence.hypothesis: Users want immediate feedback without animation delays—typing shows results instantly rather than with fade-in transitions
evidence.implication: The app should prioritize snappy, zero-latency interactions; people who hate cooking need friction removed immediately

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking respond better to a personality that acknowledges their pain while staying light—mixing genuine help with a wink
facade.label: Cheerful and sarcastic
facade.acceptImplies: Voice should be witty and conspiratorial; the app becomes a friend who gets why they're here, not a coach telling them what to do
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward warm but straightforward help; keep personality subtle and supportive rather than jokey or self-aware

facade.hypothesis: For an app targeting 'people who hate cooking', extreme simplicity and breathing room reduce overwhelm and cognitive load
facade.label: Minimalist white space
facade.acceptImplies: User values calm, unfussy interfaces that strip away noise; likely responds well to spacious layouts and deliberate restraint
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer densely-packed information, visual richness, or design that feels lively rather than austere; could indicate preference for warmer, more populated surfaces

facade.hypothesis: People who hate cooking prefer a simple, scannable ingredient checklist over narrative recipe prose
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs. key metrics — ingredient presentation format
facade.label: Just the ingredients list
facade.acceptImplies: Build around ingredient-first design: cards, checkboxes, quantity-focused. Minimize story/context.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want narrative scaffolding alongside ingredients — step-by-step guidance, why each ingredient matters, or contextual flavor notes are necessary friction reducers.

facade.hypothesis: A recipe app for cooking-averse users needs rapid gratification and minimal friction — showing results (a meal) before investment (reading instructions). This tests whether the mood should feel fast-paced and transactional or patient and guided.
facade.axisTargeted: Energetic vs Calm (secondary: Organic vs Digital)
facade.label: One-tap dinners
facade.acceptImplies: Users want snappy, efficient, results-first interactions. Design should feel zippy, modern, stripped-down. Minimal explanation. Big actions.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want a slower on-ramp. They need reassurance, context, hand-holding. Design should feel grounded, thoughtful, less rushed — even if the goal is quick meals.

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking want friction-free access to recipes—single interaction to see what to make, minimal scrolling or navigation layers.
facade.label: One-tap recipes
facade.acceptImplies: Favor flat, direct pathways. Minimize modal layers, drill-downs, and sequential steps. Prioritize speed-of-access over visual hierarchy.
facade.rejectImplies: Users expect traditional structure—category browsers, filters, search-first flows. Interaction directness is secondary to discoverability and context.

axes.label: Whether user wants visual simplicity/minimalism vs. rich imagery/inspiration; reveals tolerance for food photography and visual complexity
Whether user prefers guided step-by-step flows vs. ingredient-first browsing; reveals navigation preference for cooking-averse users
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs. straightforward/no-nonsense tone; reveals personality fit for cooking-resistant audience
Whether user wants to feel empowered/capable vs. relieved/off-the-hook; reveals emotional need driving the app choice
axes.poleA: Feel like a chef or skip the stress?

axes.label: Whether user wants detailed explanations/education vs. quick-scan essentials; reveals information density tolerance for busy/impatient users
Whether user wants smooth animations/delight vs. instant/snappy feedback; reveals patience for interface flourish vs. speed preference

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants visual simplicity/minimalism vs. rich imagery/inspiration; reveals tolerance for food photography and visual complexity
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers guided step-by-step flows vs. ingredient-first browsing; reveals navigation preference for cooking-averse users
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs. straightforward/no-nonsense tone; reveals personality fit for cooking-resistant audience
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants to feel empowered/capable vs. relieved/off-the-hook; reveals emotional need driving the app choice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants detailed explanations/education vs. quick-scan essentials; reveals information density tolerance for busy/impatient users
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants smooth animations/delight vs. instant/snappy feedback; reveals patience for interface flourish vs. speed preference
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users want to browse recipe components with fluid, swiping gestures rather than scrolling or dropdown selections—treating ingredient discovery like a tactile, card-based experience
facade.label: Swipe through ingredients
facade.acceptImplies: The app should use horizontal swiping, dragging, and gesture-based navigation for recipe filtering and ingredient browsing; people who hate cooking respond to physical, intuitive motion over click-heavy menus
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer conventional dropdown menus, buttons, and list scrolling; gesture-heavy interfaces feel gimmicky and slow down people already frustrated with cooking complexity

draft.title: QuickBite – Recipe App for People Who Hate Cooking
draft.summary: A recipe app that strips away complexity and delivers only the easiest, fastest meals with minimal ingredients and steps. Perfect for busy people who want to eat well without the cooking hassle.
draft.html:
QuickBite

Meals so easy, you'll actually cook them

Filter by effort
Under 15 min
5 ingredients
No oven
One pan
Today's easiest meals
Garlic butter pasta
12 min • 4 ingredients
Sheet pan chicken & veg
25 min • 6 ingredients
Egg fried rice
10 min • 5 ingredients
